title: astragalus episcopus
intro: Astragalus episcopus, also known as the bishop's gage or bishop's bean, is a type of plant in the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and improving immune function, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory and cardiovascular diseases. In herbal preparations, Astragalus episcopus is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and capsules that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
